studyName: Longitudinal Study of Fluoride and Other Factors Related to Dental Fluorosis, Dental Caries, and Bone Health

tables.description: DHQ Questionnaire: Responses to the Diet History Questionnaire II (DHQ II). The Iowa Bone Development Study (IBDS) utilized one of the 4 versions of the questionnaire that asks about intake in the past month and includes questions about portion size.

tables.description: DHQ Questionnaire: The file contains nutritional analysis outcomes derived from DHQ II and Healthy Eating Index (HEI) scores. The Diet*Calc application was used to generate average daily nutrient estimates based on the food frequency data collected on the DHQ forms. The HEI total score and its components were calculated and added to the file.

tables.description: Children Lifestyle Questionnaire: Responses to Lifestyle Questionnaires for Waves 7-10 regarding behaviors that might influence PA, dietary intake, and overall health of participants. For PA part of this questionnaire, most of the questions from CHOICES Questionnaire were included.

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Age at questionnaire date (rounded to 0.1 yrs)

variables.description: I think I can be physically active, even if I have a lot of homework

tables.description: Dietary Behaviors Questionnaire Interview: Questionnaire data collected during Wave 4, Wave 5, Wave 6, Wave 7, and Wave 9. The data include information on beverage exposure, meal patterns, and eating disorders. The data have been modified to provide all frequencies in the units of servings per week and all occurrances in occurances per week.

tables.description: Recruitment Questionnaire: Questionnaire data from the recruitment questionnaire that was given to mothers postpartum at the hospital. The data include information on their new baby (i.e., DOB, sex, birth weight and length), the source and filtration system for water from their kitchen faucet; their plans for feeding their baby (bottle vs. tap water and breastfeeding); the amount and type (bottled vs. tap) of water they usually drank during their pregnancy; the frequency and amount of prescription fluoride tablets taken; toothbrushing frequency; amounts of toothpaste used and ingested; demographic information for themselves and their partners; personal smoking habits before, during, and after pregnancy; and smoking habits of those in the home.

tables.description: Dental Exams: Person-level dental exam data (permanent teeth only) from Wave 2, Wave 4, Wave 6, and Wave 9. Data includes the number of non-missing surfaces for each participant, the sum of the number of surfaces with each type of lesion (D0, D1, D2+, and F) for each participant, the D2+FS count of all tooth surfaces, the D2+MFS count of all tooth surfaces, the D2+FS and D2+MFS increments (crude caries increments, net caries increments, and adjusted caries increments), age of the participant at the caries exam, person-level malocclusion data, and data on the plaque index and gingival index.

variables.name: Antpost13
variables.description: Assessment of jaw alignment in the anterior-posterior (front/back) plane at the age 13 exam. Participants with > 4 millimeters of overjet (the front teeth of the upper jaw are excessively forward of the front teeth of the lower jaw) are classified as having ">4mm overjet" or "severe overjet" while participants with a front teeth of the upper jaw behind the front teeth of the lower jaw  are classified as having a cross bite ("x-bite"), as described above.

variables.name: Antpost17
variables.description: Assessment of jaw alignment in the anterior-posterior (front/back) plane at the age 17 exam. Participants with > 4 millimeters of overjet (the front teeth of the upper jaw are excessively forward of the front teeth of the lower jaw) are classified as having ">4mm overjet" or "severe overjet" while participants with a front teeth of the upper jaw behind the front teeth of the lower jaw  are classified as having a cross bite ("x-bite"), as described above.

variables.name: Antpost23
variables.description: Assessment of jaw alignment in the anterior-posterior (front/back) plane at the age 23 exam. Participants with > 4 millimeters of overjet (the front teeth of the upper jaw are excessively forward of the front teeth of the lower jaw) are classified as having ">4mm overjet" or "severe overjet" while participants with a front teeth of the upper jaw behind the front teeth of the lower jaw  are classified as having a cross bite ("x-bite"), as described above.

variables.name: Antpost9
variables.description: Assessment of jaw alignment in the anterior-posterior (front/back) plane at the age 9 exam. Participants with > 4 millimeters of overjet (where the permanent front teeth of the upper jaw are excessively forward of the permanent front teeth of the lower jaw) are classified as having ">4mm overjet" or "severe overjet" while participants with the front teeth of the upper jaw behind the front teeth of the lower jaw  are classified as having an anterior cross bite ("x-bite").

variables.name: D0_sum13
variables.description: Number of permanent surfaces with a D0 lesion (questionable/arrested decay) for the participant at the age 13 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D0 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: transver13
variables.description: Assessment of jaw alignment in the transverse (left/right) plane at the age 13 exam. Participants with any instance of the lower teeth being positioned to the buccal (facial) to the opposing upper tooth were classified as having cross bite ("x-bite") - a  misalignment between the upper and lower jaw.

variables.name: transver17
variables.description: Assessment of jaw alignment in the transverse (left/right) plane at the 17 exam. Participants with any instance of the lower teeth being positioned to the buccal (facial) to the opposing upper tooth were classified as having cross bite ("x-bite") - a  misalignment between the upper and lower jaw.

variables.name: transver23
variables.description: Assessment of jaw alignment in the transverse (left/right) plane at the age 23 exam. Participants with any instance of the lower teeth being positioned to the buccal (facial) to the opposing upper tooth were classified as having cross bite ("x-bite") - a  misalignment between the upper and lower jaw.

variables.name: transver9
variables.description: Assessment of jaw alignment in the transverse (left/right) plane at the age 9 exam. Participants with any instance of the lower permanent or primary teeth being positioned to the buccal (facial) to the opposing upper tooth were classified as having a posterior cross bite ("x-bite") - a  misalignment between the upper and lower jaw.

variables.name: vertical13
variables.description: Assessment of the amount of vertical overlap between the upper and lower jaws at the age 13 exam. Participants with any open bite (the teeth do not have any vertical overlap between the jaws) are classified as "open" while participants with greater than 90% of the maxillary anterior teeth overlapping the lower teeth were classified as having deep bite (the teeth have excessive vertical overlap).

variables.name: vertical17
variables.description: Assessment of the amount of vertical overlap between the upper and lower jaws at the age 17 exam. Participants with any open bite (the teeth do not have any vertical overlap between the jaws) are classified as "open" while participants with greater than 90% of the maxillary anterior teeth overlapping the lower teeth were classified as having deep bite (the teeth have excessive vertical overlap).

variables.name: vertical23
variables.description: Assessment of the amount of vertical overlap between the upper and lower jaws at the age 23 exam. Participants with any open bite (the teeth do not have any vertical overlap between the jaws) are classified as "open" while participants with greater than 90% of the maxillary anterior teeth overlapping the lower teeth were classified as having deep bite (the teeth have excessive vertical overlap).

variables.name: vertical9
variables.description: Assessment of the amount of vertical overlap between the upper and lower jaws at the age 9 exam. Participants with any open bite (fully-erupted permanent teeth that do not have any vertical overlap between the jaws) are classified as "open" while participants with greater than 90% of the maxillary anterior permanent teeth overlapping the lower teeth were classified as having deep bite (the teeth have excessive vertical overlap).

variables.name: intNoHighSchoolSports
variables.description: Question 15 of Wave 6 PA Questionnaire (for each sport from the list). Please mark which, if any, High School athletics you currently participate in or have participated in. Put the number of years in each type of athletic team (freshman, sophomore, junior varsity (JV), varsity reserve, and/or varsity). For example, if you played junior varsity tennis for 2 years and varsity tennis for 1 year, put a 2 in the junior varsity tennis box and a 1 in the varsity tennis box. If you did not participate, mark 'No high school sports' No High School Sports

tables.description: Block Food Frequency Questionnaire: Data collected from the Block Kid's FFQ questionnaire that was distributed to participants in Wave 2, Wave 3, Wave 4, Wave 5, and Wave 6. The data include estimates of the participant's daily vitamin, mineral, and nutrient intakes. Estimates were calculated by Nutrion Quest using their propietary algorithm.

variables.name: PCT_LARG
variables.description: % foods marked large. On the Block's FFQ, food amounts generally had four categories. tables.description: This cross-sectional dataset contains information on the mothers' education, employment status, and ethnicity. In addition, information on the family history of cancer and osteoporosis, alcohol usage, tobacco usage, and medication usage is provided. Mothers also provided information on health conditions, age they began menstrating, number of children, if they breast fed their children, oral contraceptive usage, and if they've ever had a hysterctomy and/or ovaries removed, and if they've had breast cancer.

tables.description: Dental Exams: Person-level fluorosis, hypoplasia, and opacity data (permanent teeth only) from the Wave 2, Wave 4, Wave 6, and Wave 9 dental exams. Data includes number of tooth zones which were scored in the participant's mouth (over the entire mouth, late-erupting teeth only, early-erupting teeth only, and maxillary incisors only); number of zones with definitive fluorosis (FRI >= 2) over the entire mouth; mean FRI score for all zones in late-erupting teeth only, early-erupting teeth only, maxillary incisors only; indicator for any definitive fluorosis in the participant's mouth (FRI >=2); Dean's index; number of teeth which were scored for fluorosis, number of teeth with hypoplasia, number of teeth which were scored for opacity or hypoplasia, maximum FRI score for 2nd highest FRI tooth (over the entire mouth, late-erupting teeth only, early-erupting teeth only, maxillary incisors only).

tables.description: Oral Health Related Quality of Life Questionnaire: Questionnaire data from the Oral Health-Related Quality of Life questionnaire that was given to parents in Wave 6. The data characterize parents' feelings on the condition of their teen's teeth, lips, mouth, and jaws and how the condition impacted their life. The data also include data from a visual analog scale parents used to rate their teen's overall oral health-related quality of life. Calculated variables include oral health-related quality of life subscale scores (oral symptoms, functional limitations, emotional well-being, and social well-being) along with the overall oral health-related quality of life score.

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Age of the parent at the time of the questionnaire

variables.name: EmotionalWellBeing
variables.description: Calculated oral health-related quality of life subscale score emotional well-being. The following variables were used in the calculation: intIrritable, intShy, intConcerned, intGoodLooking, intDifferent, intUpset, intAnxious, intNotAsHealthy. See the accompanying user's guide for details on the calculation.

variables.name: FunctionalLimitations
variables.description: Calculated oral health-related quality of life subscale score for functional limitations. The following variables were used in the calculation: intMouthBreathing, intLongMeal, intTroubleSleeping, intDifficultyChewing, intTroubleSpeaking, intLikedFoods, intHotOrCold. See the accompanying user's guide for details on the calculation.

variables.name: OralSymptoms
variables.description: Calculated oral health-related quality of life subscale score for oral symptoms. The following variables were used in the calculation: intPain, intBleedingGums, intMouthSores, intBadBreath, intFoodCaught. See the accompanying user's guide for details on the calculation.

variables.name: OverallQOLScore
variables.description: Calculated overall oral health-related quality of life score. See accompanying user's guide for details on the calcution of this score.

variables.name: ParentHealthRating
variables.description: On the scale below, please rate the impact of "how your teenager's teeth look and feel" on their quality of life in performing usual and social activities that would be expected for his/her age such as eating, speaking, studying, recreation, and participating in school activities, and on his/her overall physical and mental well-being, including the level of comfort, pain, self-esteem, anxiety, or depression. The scale extends from 0 to 100, where 0 represents the worst health state that you can imagine, and 100 represents "perfect" health.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SocialWellBeing
variables.description: Calculated oral health-related quality of life subscale score social well-being. The following variables were used in the calculation: intMissedSchool, intPayingAttention, intSpeakOutLoud, intNotParticipate, intTalkToOthers, intAvoidSmiling, intNotWanted, intTeased, intLeftOut, intAskedQuestions. See the accompanying user's guide for details on the calculation.

variables.name: intAnxious
variables.description: During the past 3 months, because of his/her teeth, lips, mouth or jaws, how often has your teen been anxious or fearful?

variables.name: intAppearance
variables.description: Are you satisfied with the appearance of your teen's front teeth?

tables.description: This dataset contains information on the parents' feelings regarding the appearance of their child's teeth. They were asked about the shape, color, alignment, spacing, crowding, color irregularities, or any general things that concerned them about their child's teeth. This data is longitudinal (Waves 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 9).

tables.description: This cross-sectional dataset contains information on the fathers' education, employment status, and ethnicity. In addition, information on the family history of cancer and osteoporosis, alcohol usage, tobacco usage, and medication usage is provided. Fathers also provided information on health conditions.

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Age of participant at the time the questionnaire was completed

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: intQ1_2HighestEducation
variables.description: What is your highest level of education completed?

variables.name: intQ1_3EmploymentStatus
variables.description: What is your employment status?

variables.name: intQ1_4EthnicBackground
variables.description: What is your ethnic background?

variables.name: intQ2_1HeightFeet
variables.description: How tall were you when you were 18 years of age?

variables.name: intQ2_1HeightInches
variables.description: How tall were you when you were 18 years of age?

variables.name: intQ2_2Weight
variables.description: What did you weigh at 18 years of age?

variables.name: intQ2_3MostWeighed
variables.description: What is the most you ever weighed in pounds?

variables.name: intQ2_3aAgeAtMostWeight
variables.description: At what age did you weigh the most?

variables.name: intQ2_3bMostWeightMonths
variables.description: How long did you weigh the most?

variables.name: intQ2_3bMostWeightYears
variables.description: How long did you weigh the most?

variables.name: intQ3_1MotherHadBreastCancer
variables.description: Did your natural biological mother ever have breast cancer?

variables.name: intQ3_2FemaleRelativesHadBreastC
variables.description: Did any other female relatives (sisters, aunts, or grandmothers) ever have breast cancer?

variables.name: intQ3_3MotherHadOsteoporosis
variables.description: Did your natural biological mother ever have osteoporosis?

variables.name: intQ3_4FemaleRelativesHadOsteopo
variables.description: Did any other female relatives (sisters, aunts, or grandmother) ever have osteoporosis?

variables.name: intQ4_1DrinkAlcohol
variables.description: Have you ever drunk wine, beer, liquor, or any other drink containing alcohol?

variables.name: intQ4_1_1aAgeAtFirstDrink
variables.description: If answered "Yes" to intQ4_1DrinkAlcohol, at what age was your first drink?

variables.name: intQ4_2AlcoholInPast12Months
variables.description: In the past 12 months, have you had at least one drink of an alcoholic beverage?

variables.name: intQ4_3AlcoholFrequencyInPast30D
variables.description: Which of these statements comes closest to describing how often you drank any alcoholic beverages in the past 30 days?

variables.name: intQ4_4AlcoholAmountPerDayInPast
variables.description: During the past 30 days, how many drinks did you usually have on days when you drank alcoholic beverages (Answer for all types of alcoholic bevarages together)

variables.name: intQ5_1EverSmokedCigarettes
variables.description: Have you ever smoked cigarettes (more than a few times)?

variables.name: intQ5_1_2aCigarettesPerDay
variables.description: If answered "Current Smoker" to intQ5_1EverSmokedCigarettes, how many cigarettes do you usually smoke per day?

variables.name: intQ5_1_2bAgeStartedSmoking
variables.description: If answered "Current Smoker" to intQ5_1EverSmoked, at what age did you start smoking?

variables.name: intQ5_1_3aYearsSmokedCigarettes
variables.description: If answered "Former Smoker" to intQ5_1EverSmokedCigarettes, how many years did you smoke cigarettes?

variables.name: intQ5_1_3bAgeStartedSmoking
variables.description: If answered "Former Smoker" to intQ5_1EverSmokedCigarettes, at what age did you start smoking?

variables.name: intQ5_1_3cAgeStoppedSmoking
variables.description: If answered "Former Smoker" to intQ5_1EverSmokedCigarettes, at what age did you permanently stop smoking?

variables.name: intQ5_1_3dCigarettesPerDay
variables.description: If answered "Former Smoker" to intQ5_1EverSmokedCigarettes, how many cigarettes did you usually smoke a day?

variables.name: intQ6_10CholesterolLoweringDrugs
variables.description: Are you taking cholesterol lowering drugs (e.g., Lipitor, Mevacor, Zocor, Pravachol, Baycol and other Statin medications) to lower cholesterol?

variables.name: intQ6_11ThyroidHormonePills
variables.description: Are you taking thyroid hormone pills (e.g., levothyroxine or methimazole for hypothyroidism; propythiuracil for hyperthyroidism)?

variables.name: intQ6_1Anticoagulants
variables.description: Are you taking anticoagulants (e.g., coumadin) to thin your blood?

variables.name: intQ6_2Corticosteroids
variables.description: Are you taking corticosteroids (e.g., prednisolone) (Not aspirin, Tylenol, ibuprofen) to ease chronic inflammation?

variables.name: intQ6_3Anticonvulsants
variables.description: Are you taking anticonvulsants to stop seizures?

variables.name: intQ6_4AntiOsteoporosisDrugs

tables.description: Oral Health Related Quality of Life Questionnaire: Questionnaire data from the Oral Health-Related Quality of Life questionnaire that was given to participants in Wave 6, Wave 7, and Wave 9. The data characterize participants' feelings on the condition of their teeth, lips, mouth, and jaws and how the condition impacted their life. The data also include data from a visual analog scale participants used to rate their overall oral health-related quality of life. Calculated variables include oral health-related quality of life subscale scores (oral symptoms, functional limitations, emotional well-being, and social well-being) along with the overall oral health-related quality of life score.

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Subject's age in years (rounded to tenths) at the time the questionnaire was answered

variables.name: EmotionalWellBeing
variables.description: Calculated oral health-related quality of life subscale score for emotional well-being. The following variables were used in the calculation: intIrritable, intShy, intConcerned, intGoodLooking, intDifferent, intUpset, intNervous, intNotAsHealthy. See the accompanying user's guide for details on the calculation.

variables.name: FunctionalLimitations
variables.description: Calculated oral health-related quality of life subscale score for functional limitations. The following variables were used in the calculation: intMouthBreathing, intLongMeal, intTroubleSleeping, intDifficultyChewing, intTroubleSpeaking, intLikedFoods, intHotOrCold. See the accompanying user's guide for details on the calculation.

variables.name: OralSymptoms
variables.description: Calculated oral health-related quality of life subscale score for oral symptoms. The following variables were used in the calculation: intPain, intBleedingGums, intMouthSores, intBadBreath, intFoodCaught. See the accompanying user's guide for details on the calculation.

variables.name: OverallQoLScore
variables.description: Calculated overall oral health-related quality of life score. See accompanying user's guide for details on the calcution of this score.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SocialWellBeing
variables.description: Calculated oral health-related quality of life subscale score for social well-being. The following variables were used in the calculation: intMissedSchool, intPayingAttention, intSpeakOutLoud, intNotParticipate, intTalkToOthers, intAvoidSmiling, intSpendTime, intTeased, intLeftOut, intAskedQuestions. See the accompanying user's guide for details on the calculation.

variables.name: TeenHealthRating
variables.description: On the scale below, please rate the impact of "how your teeth look and feel" on your quality of life in performing usual daily activities such as eating, speaking, exercising, studying, working (if applicable), and social interactions, and on your overall physical and mental well-being, including the level of comfort, pain, self-esteem, anxiety, or depression. The scale extends from 0 to 100, where 0 represents the worst health state that you can imagine, and 100 represents "perfect" health.

variables.name: WAVE

tables.description: Parent Fluoride: Questionnaire data from the Parent's Fluoride Intake: Current questionnaire. The data include where the water in their kitchen faucet/tap came from; if a water softener was used; what type of water filtration or purification system they used (if any); their habits regarding drinking tap vs. bottled water; how much water they drank a day; where the water they drank throughout the day came from (home, work, or other); if they took prescription fluoride drops, tablets, or vitamins with fluoride; how often they brushed their teeth with toothpaste; what brand of toothpaste they used; how much toothpaste they used; how much toothpaste they usually swallowed; if they used a fluoride mouth rinse or gel; which fluoride mouth rinse or gel they used; how often they used the fluoride mouth rinse or gel; how much of the fluoride mouth rinse or gel was usually swallowed; if they had a dental (or dental hygiene) appointment; and if they received a professional (office) fluoride treatment during the previous 6 months.

tables.description: Parent Fluoride: Questionnaire data from the Father's Fluoride Intake: Past questionnaire. The data include where fathers lived during the pregnancy (with the participant's mother or without); the type of water system and filtration system at their different addresses during the pregnancy; if they drank mostly tap water, mostly bottled water, or equal amounts of tap and bottled water; the amount of 8-ounce glasses of water or beverages made with water they consumed on average per day at home, work, and other locations; if they took any prescription fluoride tablets; how often they brushed their teeth with toothpaste; the brand of toothpaste used; the amount of toothpaste they used; and the amount of toothpaste they usually swallowed.

variables.description: Age of the participant at the time the questionnaire was given

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID

tables.description: Dental Exams: Zone-level dental exam data (permanent teeth only) from Wave 2, Wave 4, Wave 6, and Wave 9. The occlusal surface on maxillary molars was seperated into two zones: occlusal mesial and occlusal distal. Data includes the numeric caries code for permanent dentition, caries code translated into character "status" variable which indicates status of decay and/or fillings, indicator for any sealant, indicator for whether any sealant is fully retained, indicator for whether lesions are separate or recurrent for any fillings or sealants, indicator for whether the zone has a cavitated lesion or filling, indicator for whether the zone is missing due to caries, indicator for whether the zone is missing due to reasons other than caries, indicator for whether the zone is primarily a pit and fissure surface, indicator for whether lesion present on the zone is located on a pit and fissure or smooth portion of the zone.

variables.name: Caries13
variables.description: Numeric code for caries scores for each permanent tooth surface/zone at age 13. Please see the accompanying user guide for the key to the code system.

variables.name: Caries17
variables.description: Numeric code for caries scores for each permanent tooth surface/zone at age 17. Please see the accompanying user guide for the key to the code system.

variables.name: Caries23
variables.description: Numeric code for caries scores for each permanent tooth surface/zone at age 23. Please see the accompanying user guide for the key to the code system.

variables.name: Caries9
variables.description: Numeric code for caries scores for each permanent tooth surface/zone at age 9. Please see the accompanying user guide for the key to the code system.

variables.name: PitFissureLesion13
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a pit and fissure portion of a permanent tooth surface/zone at the age 13 exam.

variables.name: PitFissureLesion17
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a pit and fissure portion of a permanent tooth surface/zone at the age 17 exam.

variables.name: PitFissureLesion23
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a pit and fissure portion of a permanent tooth surface/zone at the age 23 exam.

variables.name: PitFissureSurface
variables.description: Indicator for whether a permanent tooth surface/zone is a pit and fissure surface/zone.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SmoothLesion13
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a smooth portion of a permanent tooth surface/zone at the age 13 exam.

variables.name: SmoothLesion17
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a smooth portion of a permanent tooth surface/zone at the age 17 exam.

variables.name: SmoothLesion23
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a smooth portion of a permanent tooth surface/zone at the age 23 exam.

variables.name: SmoothLesion9
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a smooth portion of a permanent tooth surface/zone at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: Surface
variables.description: Surface/zone label. In this data set, the occlusal surface of the maxillary molars (teeth 1,2,3,14,15,and 16) is divided into the occlusal medial (OM) or occlusal distal (OD) zones.

variables.name: Tooth
variables.description: Permanent tooth number, ranging from 1 to 32. Although the 3rd molars were scored at the age 23 year exam, they were not present to be scored for most participants. For the age 9 exam, only permanent teeth scored are included in this dataset.

tables.description: Dental Exams: Tooth-level dental exam data (permanent teeth only) from Wave 2, Wave 4, Wave 6, and Wave 9. Data includes the count of the number of non-missing surfaces on the tooth, the sum of the number of tooth surfaces with the different lesions (D0, D1, D2+, F) the sum of the number of surfaces on the tooth which were not scored due to reasons other than caries, D2+FS count of surfaces on the tooth, D2+MFS count of surfaces on the tooth, indicator for whether all surfaces are missing/not scored on the tooth for any reason, indicator for whether the tooth is missing due to caries.

variables.name: D0_sum13
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D0 lesion (questionable/arrested decay) on the permanent tooth at the age 13 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D0 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D0_sum17
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D0 lesion (questionable/arrested decay) on the permanent tooth at the age 17 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D0 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D0_sum23
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D0 lesion (questionable/arrested decay) on the permanent tooth at the age 23 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D0 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D0_sum9
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D0 lesion (questionable/arrested decay) on the permanent tooth at the age 9 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D0 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D1_sum13
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D1 lesion (incipient decay/non-cavitated caries) on the permanent tooth at the age 13 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D1 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D1_sum17
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D1 lesion (incipient decay/non-cavitated caries) on the permanent tooth at the age 17 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D1 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D1_sum23
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D1 lesion (incipient decay/non-cavitated caries) on the permanent tooth at the age 23 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D1 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D1_sum9
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D1 lesion (incipient decay/non-cavitated caries) on the permanent tooth at the age 9 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D1 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D2FS_count13
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which have cavitated caries (D2 lesion) and/or a filling at the age 13 exam (tooth-level decayed or filled surface count).

variables.name: D2FS_count17
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which have cavitated caries (D2 lesion) and/or a filling at the age 17 exam (tooth-level decayed or filled surface count).

variables.name: D2FS_count23
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which have cavitated caries (D2 lesion) and/or a filling at the age 23 exam (tooth-level decayed or filled surface count).

variables.name: D2FS_count9
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which have cavitated caries (D2 lesion) and/or a filling at the age 9 exam (tooth-level decayed or filled surface count).

variables.name: D2MFS_count13
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which have cavitated caries (D2 lesion), are missing due to caries, and/or have a filling at the age 13 exam (tooth-level decyaed or filled surface count).

variables.name: D2MFS_count17
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which have cavitated caries (D2 lesion), are missing due to caries, and/or have a filling at the age 17 exam (tooth-level decyaed or filled surface count).

variables.name: D2MFS_count23
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which have cavitated caries (D2 lesion), are missing due to caries, and/or have a filling at the age 23 exam (tooth-level decyaed or filled surface count).

variables.name: D2MFS_count9
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which have cavitated caries (D2 lesion), are missing due to caries, and/or have a filling at the age 9 exam (tooth-level decyaed or filled surface count).

variables.name: D2_sum13
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D2 lesion (frank/cavitated caries) on the permanent tooth at the age 13 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D2 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D2_sum17
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D2 lesion (frank/cavitated caries) on the permanent tooth at the age 17 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D2 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D2_sum23
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D2 lesion (frank/cavitated caries) on the permanent tooth at the age 23 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D2 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: D2_sum9
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a D2 lesion (frank/cavitated caries) on the permanent tooth at the age 9 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated D2 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: F_sum13
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a filling on the permanent tooth at the age 13 exam, regardless of whether or not the surface had separate or recurrent caries lesions.

variables.name: F_sum17
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a filling on the permanent tooth at the age 17 exam, regardless of whether or not the surface had separate or recurrent caries lesions.

variables.name: F_sum23
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a filling on the permanent tooth at the age 23 exam, regardless of whether or not the surface had separate or recurrent caries lesions.

variables.name: F_sum9
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a filling on the permanent tooth at the age 9 exam, regardless of whether or not the surface had separate or recurrent caries lesions.

variables.name: Missing_caries13
variables.description: Indicator for whether the permanent tooth is missing due to caries at the age 13 exam.

variables.name: Missing_caries17
variables.description: Indicator for whether the permanent tooth is missing due to caries at the age 17 exam.

variables.name: Missing_caries23
variables.description: Indicator for whether the permanent tooth is missing due to caries at the age 23 exam.

variables.name: Missing_caries9
variables.description: Indicator for whether the permanent tooth is missing due to caries at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: Missing_non_caries_sum13
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which were missing/not scored due to reasons other than caries at the age 13 exam.

variables.name: Missing_non_caries_sum17
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which were missing/not scored due to reasons other than caries at the age 17 exam.

variables.name: Missing_non_caries_sum23
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which were missing/not scored due to reasons other than caries at the age 23 exam.

variables.name: Missing_non_caries_sum9
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the permanent tooth which were missing/not scored due to reasons other than caries at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: Tooth
variables.description: Permanent tooth number, ranging from 1 to 32. Although the 3rd molars were scored at the age 23 year exam, they were not present to be scored for most participants. For the age 9 exam, only permanent teeth scored are included in this dataset.

variables.name: all_surfs_missing13
variables.description: Indicator for whether all surfaces are missing for the permanent tooth at the age 13 exam.

variables.name: all_surfs_missing17
variables.description: Indicator for whether all surfaces are missing for the permanent tooth at the age 17 exam.

variables.name: all_surfs_missing23
variables.description: Indicator for whether all surfaces are missing for the permanent tooth at the age 23 exam.

variables.name: all_surfs_missing9
variables.description: Indicator for whether all surfaces are missing for the permanent tooth at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: non_missing_sum13
variables.description: Number of non-missing (scored) surfaces on the permanent tooth at the age 13 exam.

variables.name: non_missing_sum17
variables.description: Number of non-missing (scored) surfaces on the permanent tooth at the age 17 exam.

variables.name: non_missing_sum23
variables.description: Number of non-missing (scored) surfaces on the permanent tooth at the age 23 exam.

variables.name: non_missing_sum9
variables.description: Number of non-missing (scored) surfaces on the permanent tooth at the age 9 exam.

tables.description: This dataset contains information on the parents' current (at the time of questionnaire) and past (during high school) food habits. Parents were asked to list number of servings for different broad categories of food such as milk, coffee, tea, soda, alcoholic beverages, cheese, leafy greans, etc. This was not a longitudinal questionnaire; parents were only asked to fill out this information at a single time point.

tables.description: DXA Children: Bone strength outcomes from Hip Structural Analysis (HSA) in hip sub-regions (femoral neck, intertrochanteric, and femur shaft) for study assessment Waves 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 and 9.

variables.name: FS_ACT
variables.description: Femur Shaft Average Cortical Thickness: (FS_WIDTH - FS_ED) / 2.

variables.name: FS_BMD
variables.description: Femur Shaft Bone Mineral Density: (FS_CSA / FS_WIDTH) * 1.05.

variables.name: FS_BR
variables.description: Intertrochanter Buckling Ratio (dimensionless): (FS_CSMI / FS_SECT_MOD) / FS_ACT.

variables.name: FS_CMP
variables.description: Femur Shaft Center of Mass Position (dimensionless): FS_PCD / FS_WIDTH.

variables.name: FS_CSA
variables.description: Femur Shaft Cross Sectional Area: (sum of pixel values in profile)*(pixel spacing along profile/1.05).

variables.name: FS_CSMI
variables.description: Femur Shaft Cross Sectional Moment of Inertia: (sum of pixel mass at each point in profile times square of its distance from profile center of mass)*(pixel spacing along profile/1.05).

variables.name: FS_ED
variables.description: Femur Shaft Endocortical Diameter: 2*((FS_WIDTH / 2)**2 - (0.6 * FS_CSA / pi))**0.5.

variables.name: FS_PCD
variables.description: Femur Shaft Profile Center Distance: Distance from profile center of mass to medial margin of cortex.

variables.name: FS_SECT_MOD
variables.description: Femur Shaft Section Modulus : If (FS_CMP >= 0.5) then (FS_CSMI / FS_PCD) else (FS_CSMI / (FS_WIDTH - FS_PCD)).

variables.name: FS_WIDTH
variables.description: Femur Shaft Subperiosteal Width: Blur-corrected width of the mass profile.

variables.name: IT_ACT
variables.description: Intertrochanter Average Cortical Thickness: (IT_WIDTH - IT_ED) / 2

variables.name: IT_BMD
variables.description: Intertrochanter Bone Mineral Density: (IT_CSA / IT_WIDTH) * 1.05.

variables.name: IT_BR
variables.description: Intertrochanter Buckling Ratio (dimensionless): (IT_CSMI / IT_SECT_MOD) / IT_ACT.

variables.name: IT_CMP
variables.description: Intertrochanter Center of Mass Position (dimensionless): IT_PCD / IT_WIDTH.

variables.name: IT_CSMI
variables.description: Intertrochanter Cross Sectional Moment of Inertia: (sum of pixel mass at each point in profile times square of its distance from profile center of mass)*(pixel spacing along profile/1.05).

variables.name: IT_ED
variables.description: Intertrochanter Endocortical Diameter: 2*((IT_WIDTH / 2)**2 - (0.6 * IT_CSA / pi))**0.5.

variables.name: IT_PCD
variables.description: Intertrochanter Profile Center Distance : Distance from profile center of mass to medial margin of cortex.

variables.name: IT_SECT_MOD
variables.description: Intertrochanter Section Modulus: If (IT_CMP >= 0.5) then (IT_CSMI/ IT_PCD) else (IT_CSMI / (IT_WIDTH - IT_PCD))

variables.name: IT_WIDTH
variables.description: Intertrochanter Subperiosteal Width: Blur-corrected width of the mass profile.

tables.description: This dataset contains information on the participants' radial and tibial pQCT cortical bone measures. This includes cortical bone mineral content, bone mineral density, bone area, bone thickness, periosteal and endosteal circumferences, and other variables.

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Age at assessment date (rounded to 0.1 yrs)

variables.name: BONE
variables.description: Tibia or radius bone scan with scan site location (% of bone length  is measured from reference line)

variables.name: CRT_A
variables.description: Cortical area in mm2. The area that is assigned to be pure cortical.

variables.name: CRT_CNT
variables.description: Cortical content per 1mm slice

variables.name: CRT_DEN
variables.description: Cortical density. The mean density of the pure cortical bone.  Because of partial volume affects, the voxels comprising the periosteal and endosteal surfaces contain an average of bone and soft tissue.  This reduces the true density of the total cortical shell

variables.name: CRT_THK
variables.description: Mean cortical thickness, defined by the average distance between outer and inner edge of the cortical shell.

variables.name: CRT_THK_C
variables.description: The cortical thickness CRT_THK_C in the "circular ring model" is the difference between the outer and the inner radius.

variables.name: CRT_THK_SD
variables.description: Cortical thickness standard deviation

variables.name: ENDO
variables.description: Endosteal circumference (length of the inner contour)

variables.name: ENDO_C
variables.description: Endosteal circumference in the "circular ring model"

variables.name: IP_CRT_A
variables.description: Polar area moment of inertia of the area - IP_CRT_A =sum

variables.name: IXY_CRT_A
variables.description: Mixed term (needed to calculate the moment of inertia for any direction in the cross-sectional plane) - IXY_CRT_A = sum[ a*(X_CRT_A-x)*(Y_CRT_A-y)], (a=area of one Voxel, sum is performed over the cortical area)

variables.name: IX_CRT_A
variables.description: Axial area moment of inertia of the cortical area, the concerning axis is parallel to the x-axis and includes the center of area - IX_CRT_A = sum[ a*(Y_CRT_A-y)2], (a=area of one Voxel, sum is performed over the cortical area)

variables.name: IY_CRT_A
variables.description: Like IX_CRT_A, the concerning axis is parallel to the y-axis - IY_CRT_A = sum[ a*(X_CRT_A-x)2], (a=area of one Voxel, sum is performed over the cortical area)

variables.name: MOVE1
variables.description: Scan quality indicator (movement defined by technician)

variables.name: OBJECTLEN
variables.description: Bone length

variables.name: PERI
variables.description: Periosteal circumference (length of the outer contour)

variables.name: PERI_C
variables.description: Periosteal circumference in the "circular ring model"

variables.name: P_Move_R
variables.description: Proportion of movement (comparison  of cortical bone areas obtained from movement loop)

variables.name: RL
variables.description: Reference line

variables.name: RP_CRT_A
variables.description: Moment of resistance = IP_CRT_A/(max distance to the center)

variables.name: RX_CRT_A
variables.description: Moment of resistance = IX_CRT_A/(max distance to y-axis)

variables.name: RY_CRT_A
variables.description: Moment of resistance = IY_CRT_A/(max distance to x-axis)

variables.name: SCANID
variables.description: Scan id (identificator of scan - same as for image file)

variables.name: SCANNER
variables.description: Scanner used

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Sex

variables.name: SIDE
variables.description: Body side  where limb scan was performed

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: THRESHOLD
variables.description: Constant for all records=710 mg/cm3 (or 711 mg/cm3 as the XCT software tends to round the set user threshold of 710 to 711, this is normal and a product of the attenuation to density conversion) is the default threshold for cortical analysis and represents half way between the density of soft tissue and cortical bone

variables.name: WAVE
variables.description: Study Assessment Wave

variables.name: X_CRT_A
variables.description: X-coordinate of the center of cortical area (mean x-coordinate of all Voxels of the cortical area)

variables.name: Y_CRT_A
variables.description: Y-coordinate of the center of cortical area (mean y-coordinate of all Voxels of the cortical area)

tables.description: Dental Exams: Tooth-level fluorosis, hypoplasia, and opacity data (permanent teeth only) from the Wave 2, Wave 4, Wave 6, and Wave 9 dental exams. Data includes number of zones which were scored on each tooth, number of zones with definitive fluorosis on the tooth, indicator for any definitive fluorosis on the tooth, highest FRI score for each tooth, average FRI score for each tooth, separate indicators for opacity and hypoplasia on the tooth.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: Tooth
variables.description: Permanent tooth number, ranging from 2 to 31. Although the 3rd molars were scored at the age 23 year exam, they were not not scored for fluorosis. For the age 9 exam, only permanent teeth scored are included in this dataset.

variables.name: any_zones_def_flu_13
variables.description: Indicator for whether or not any zones on the permanent tooth had definitive fluorosis (FRI >= 2) at age 13.

variables.name: any_zones_def_flu_17

variables.name: hypoplasia23

variables.name: max_tooth_FRI_9

variables.name: mean_FRI_13
variables.description: Average FRI scores over the zones of the permanent tooth at age 13.

variables.name: mean_FRI_17
variables.description: Average FRI scores over the zones of the permanent tooth at age 17.

variables.name: mean_FRI_23
variables.description: Average FRI scores over the zones of the permanent tooth at age 23.

variables.name: mean_FRI_9

variables.name: num_zones_def_flu_13

variables.name: num_zones_def_flu_17

tables.description: Dataset contains information on the mothers' fluoride intake from water during pregnancy. Estimated total fluoride intake from tap water, bottle water, beverages made from tap water, and beverages made from bottled water are provided. The estimated total from ingested toothpaste and from supplements along with the estimated total intake from all sources combined is provided.

variables.name: F11

tables.description: Dental Exams: Person-level dental exam data (primary teeth only) for Wave 1 and Wave 2 dental exams. Data includes age of the participant at the exam, number of tooth surfaces which are non-missing for the participant, sum of the number of tooth surfaces with the different lesions (d0, d1, d2+, f), d2+fs count for all tooth surfaces, d2+mfs count for all tooth surfaces, d2+fs and d2+mfs increments (crude caries increment, net caries increment, and adjusted caries increment), and person-level malocclusion data.

variables.name: Antpost9
variables.description: Assessment of jaw alignment in the anterior-posterior (front/back) plane at the age 9 exam. Participants with > 4 millimeters of overjet (where the permanent front teeth of the upper jaw are excessively forward of the permanent front teeth of the lower jaw) are classified as having ">4mm overjet" or "severe overjet" while participants with the front teeth of the upper jaw behind the front teeth of the lower jaw  are classified as having an anterior cross bite ("x-bite").

variables.name: D0_sum5
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces with a d0 lesion (questionable/arrested decay) for the participant at the age 5 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d0 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: d0_sum9
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces with a d0 lesion (questionable/arrested decay) for the participant at the age 9 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d0 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d1_sum5
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces with a d1 lesion (incipient decay/non-cavitated caries) for the participant at the age 5 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d1 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d1_sum9
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces with a d1 lesion (incipient decay/non-cavitated caries) for the participant at the age 9 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d1 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d2_sum5
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces with a d2 lesion (frank/cavitated caries) for the participant at the age 5 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d2 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d2_sum9
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces with a d2 lesion (frank/cavitated caries) for the participant at the age 9 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d2 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d2fs_ACI_5_9
variables.description: Adjusted caries increment for decayed (d2) or filled primary tooth surfaces from ages 5 - 9.

variables.name: d2fs_CCI_5_9
variables.description: Crude caries increment for decayed (d2) or filled primary tooth surfaces from ages 5 - 9.

variables.name: d2fs_NCI_5_9
variables.description: Net caries increment for decayed (d2) or filled primary tooth surfaces from ages 5 - 9.

variables.name: d2fs_count5
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces for the participant which have cavitated caries (d2 lesion) and/or a filling at the age 5 exam (participant-level decayed or filled surface count).

variables.name: d2fs_count9
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces for the participant which have cavitated caries (d2 lesion) and/or a filling at the age 9 exam (participant-level decayed or filled surface count).

variables.name: d2mfs_ACI_5_9
variables.description: Adjusted caries increment for decayed (d2), missing due to caries, or filled primary tooth surfaces from ages 5 - 9.

variables.name: d2mfs_CCI_5_9
variables.description: Crude caries increment for decayed (d2), missing due to caries, or filled primary tooth surfaces from ages 5 - 9.

variables.name: d2mfs_NCI_5_9
variables.description: Net caries increment for decayed (d2), missing due to caries, or filled primary tooth surfaces from ages 5 - 9.

variables.name: d2mfs_count5
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces for the participant which have cavitated caries (d2 lesion), are missing due to caries, and/or have a filling at the age 5 exam (participant-level decyaed or filled surface count).

variables.name: d2mfs_count9
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces for the participant which have cavitated caries (d2 lesion), are missing due to caries, and/or have a filling at the age 9 exam (participant-level decyaed or filled surface count).

variables.name: examage5
variables.description: Age of the participant (in years) at the time of the age 5 caries exam.

variables.name: examage9
variables.description: Age of the participant (in years) at the time of the age 9 caries exam.

variables.name: f_sum5
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces with a filling for the participant at the age 5 exam, regardless of whether or not the surface had separate or recurrent caries lesions.

variables.name: f_sum9
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces with a filling for the participant at the age 9 exam, regardless of whether or not the surface had separate or recurrent caries lesions.

variables.name: molar9
variables.description: Angle classification (levels I, II, III), corresponding to the 1st permanent molar relationship between the upper and lower jaws at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: non_missing_sum5
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces which are not missing for any reason at the age 5 exam (surfaces missing due to caries are not counted in this sum).

variables.name: non_missing_sum9
variables.description: Number of primary tooth surfaces which are not missing for any reason at the age 9 exam (surfaces missing due to caries are not counted in this sum).

variables.name: transver9
variables.description: Assessment of jaw alignment in the transverse (left/right) plane at the age 9 exam. Participants with any instance of the lower permanent or primary teeth being positioned to the buccal (facial) to the opposing upper tooth were classified as having a posterior cross bite ("x-bite") - a  misalignment between the upper and lower jaw.

variables.name: vertical9
variables.description: Assessment of the amount of vertical overlap between the upper and lower jaws at the age 9 exam. Participants with any open bite (fully-erupted permanent teeth that do not have any vertical overlap between the jaws) are classified as "open" while participants with greater than 90% of the maxillary anterior permanent teeth overlapping the lower teeth were classified as having deep bite (the teeth have excessive vertical overlap).

tables.description: Dental Exams: Surface-level dental exam data (primary teeth only) for Wave 1 and Wave 2 dental exams. Data includes numeric caries code for primary dentition, tooth status variable (not collected in Wave 1), caries code translated into character "status" variable which indicates status of decay and/or fillings which are present, indicator for any sealant on the surface, indicator for whether any sealant on the surface is fully retained (not collected at Wave 1), indicator for whether lesions are separate or recurrent for any fillings or sealant, indicator for whether the surface has a cavitated lesion or filling, indicator for whether the surface is missing due to caries, indicator for whether the surface is missing due to reasons other than caries, indicator for whether the surface is primarily a pit and fissure surface, indicator for whether any lesion present on the surface is located on a pit and fissure or smooth portion of the surface.

variables.name: Caries5
variables.description: Numeric code for caries scores for each primary tooth surface at age 5. Please see the accompanying user guide for the key to the code system.

variables.name: Caries9
variables.description: Numeric code for caries scores for each primary tooth surface at age 9. Please see the accompanying user guide for the key to the code system.

variables.name: PitFissureLesion5
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a pit and fissure portion of a primary tooth surface/zone at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: PitFissureLesion9
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a pit and fissure portion of a primary tooth surface/zone at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: PitFissureSurface
variables.description: Indicator for whether or not a surface is considered pit and fissure.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: SmoothLesion5
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a smooth portion of a primary tooth surface/zone at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: SmoothLesion9
variables.description: Indicator for whether a lesion occurred on a smooth portion of a primary tooth surface/zone at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: Surface
variables.description: Surface label for tooth.

variables.name: Tooth
variables.description: Primary tooth letter, ranging from A to T. For the age 9 exam, only primary teeth scored are included in this dataset.

variables.name: any_cavitated_filled5
variables.description: Indicator for whether the primary tooth surface/zone had any cavitated caries or fillings at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: any_cavitated_filled9
variables.description: Indicator for whether the primary tooth surface/zone had any cavitated caries or fillings at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: any_sealant5
variables.description: Indicator for any sealant on the primary tooth surface/zone (either fully or partially retained) at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: any_sealant9
variables.description: Indicator for any sealant on the primary tooth surface/zone (either fully or partially retained) at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: full_sealant9
variables.description: Indicator for whether a sealant on the primary tooth surface/zone was fully retained at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: letter_caries5
variables.description: Label for the caries/filling status of each primary tooth surface at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: letter_caries9
variables.description: Label for the caries/filling status of each primary tooth surface at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: missing_caries5
variables.description: Indicator for whether the primary tooth surface/zone was missing due to caries at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: missing_caries9
variables.description: Indicator for whether the primary tooth surface/zone was missing due to caries at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: missing_noncaries5
variables.description: Indicator for whether the primary tooth surface/zone was missing for reasons other than caries at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: missing_noncaries9
variables.description: Indicator for whether the primary tooth surface/zone was missing for reasons other than caries at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: recurrent5
variables.description: Indicator for whether a caries lesion is recurrent on a filling or sealant at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: recurrent9
variables.description: Indicator for whether a caries lesion is recurrent on a filling or sealant at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: separate5
variables.description: Indicator for whether a caries lesion is separate from a filling or sealant at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: separate9
variables.description: Indicator for whether a caries lesion is separate from a filling or sealant at the age 9 exam.

variables.description: Primary tooth status variable recorded at age 5.

tables.description: This dataset contains information on the participants' radial and tibial pQCT trabecular bone measures. This includes trabecular bone mineral content, density, and area, and Bone Strength Indices (BSI) (compressive strength) for total, cortical, and trabecular bone.

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Age at assessment date (rounded to 0.1 yrs)

variables.name: BONE
variables.description: Tibia or radius bone scan with scan site location (% of bone length is measured from reference line)

variables.name: BSI_CRT
variables.description: Bone Strength Index (compression strength) for cortical bone                                                                                                                                                                                                                                BSI= CRTSUB_DEN(mg/mm3)*CRTSUB_DEN(mg/mm3)*CRTSUB_A(mm2)

variables.name: BSI_TOT
variables.description: Bone Strength Index (compression strength) BSI_TOT= TOT_DEN(mg/mm3)*TOT_DEN(mg/mm3)*TOT_A(mm2)

variables.name: BSI_TRAB
variables.description: Bone Strength Index (compression strength) for trabecular bone BSI_TRAB= TRAB_DEN(mg/mm3)* TRAB_DEN(mg/mm3)*TRAB_A(mm2)

variables.name: CRTSUB_A
variables.description: Cortical and sub-cortical area in mm2. The area between the outer bone edge and the trabecular area. Sub cortical bone is defined as the region that could neither be assigned to cortical nor trabecular area. The Calcbd analysis finds the total area of the bone then peels into the trabecular area.  Anything within the total bone that is not trabecular is this area.

variables.name: CRTSUB_CNT
variables.description: Cortical and sub-cortical content per 1mm slice (mg/mm). The mineral content of the cortical and sub-cortical bone within a 1mm slice.

variables.name: CRTSUB_DEN
variables.description: Cortical and sub-cortical density (mg/ cm3). The mean density of the cortical and sub-cortical bone.

variables.name: MOVE1
variables.description: Scan quality (movement defined by technician)

variables.name: N_Move_A
variables.description: Negative movement area from analysis loop to quantify movement

variables.name: OBJECTLEN
variables.description: Bone length

variables.name: PERI_C
variables.description: Periosteal circumference in the "circular ring model"

variables.name: RL
variables.description: Reference line

variables.name: SCANID
variables.description: Scan id (identifier of scan-same as for image file)

variables.name: SCANNER
variables.description: Scanner used

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Sex

variables.name: SIDE
variables.description: Body side where limb scan was performed

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: TOT_A
variables.description: Total bone area in mm2. Cross-sectional area of the bone after the soft tissue has been peeled off.

variables.name: TOT_CNT
variables.description: Total bone content per 1mm slice (mg/mm). The mineral content of the total bone within a 1mm slice.

variables.name: TOT_DEN
variables.description: Total bone density. The mean density of the total bone.

variables.name: TRAB_A
variables.description: Trabecular area in mm2. Cross-sectional area of the trabecular area after the cortical and sub-cortical area has been peeled off.

variables.name: TRAB_CNT
variables.description: Trabecular content per 1mm slice (mg/mm). The mineral content of the trabecular bone within a 1mm slice.

variables.name: TRAB_DEN
variables.description: Trabecular density (mg/cm3). The mean density of the trabecular bone.  The trabecular bone is surrounded by bone marrow fat, so this is an "apparent" density of trabecular bone, as it is an average of bone and bone marrow fat.  (This also applies to TRAB_CNT, TOT_CNT, and TOT_DEN)

variables.name: WAVE
variables.description: Study assessment wave

variables.description: Was the bleaching done in your dentist's office?

tables.description: Dental Exams: Tooth-level dental exam data (primary teeth only) for Wave 1 and Wave 2 dental exams. Data includes the count of the number of non-missing surfaces on the tooth, sum of the number of tooth surfaces with the different lesions (d0, d1, d2+, f), sum of the number of surfaces on the tooth which were not scored due to reasons other than caries, d2+fs count of surfaces on the tooth, d2+mfs count of surfaces on the tooth, indicator for whether all surfaces are missing/not scored on the tooth for any reason, indicator for whether the tooth is missing due to caries.

variables.name: Missing_non_caries_sum5
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the primary tooth which were missing/not scored due to reasons other than caries at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: Missing_non_caries_sum9
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the primary tooth which were missing/not scored due to reasons other than caries at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: Tooth
variables.description: Primary tooth letter, ranging from A to T. For the age 9 exam, only primary teeth scored are included in this dataset.

variables.name: all_surfs_missing5
variables.description: Indicator for whether all surfaces are missing for the primary tooth at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: all_surfs_missing9
variables.description: Indicator for whether all surfaces are missing for the primary tooth at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: d0_sum5
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a d0 lesion (questionable/arrested decay) on the primary tooth at the age 5 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d0 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d0_sum9
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a d0 lesion (questionable/arrested decay) on the primary tooth at the age 9 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d0 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d1_sum5
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a d1 lesion (incipient decay/non-cavitated caries) on the primary tooth at the age 5 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d1 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d1_sum9
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a d1 lesion (incipient decay/non-cavitated caries) on the primary tooth at the age 9 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d1 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d2_sum5
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a d2 lesion (frank/cavitated caries) on the primary tooth at the age 5 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d2 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d2_sum9
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a d2 lesion (frank/cavitated caries) on the primary tooth at the age 9 exam. This includes surfaces with any untreated d2 lesion, which can be recurrent or separate from a filling or sealant.

variables.name: d2fs_count5
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the primary tooth which have cavitated caries (d2 lesion) and/or a filling at the age 5 exam (tooth-level decayed or filled surface count).

variables.name: d2fs_count9
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the primary tooth which have cavitated caries (d2 lesion) and/or a filling at the age 9 exam (tooth-level decayed or filled surface count).

variables.name: d2mfs_count5
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the primary tooth which have cavitated caries (d2 lesion), are missing due to caries, and/or have a filling at the age 5 exam (tooth-level decayed or filled surface count).

variables.name: d2mfs_count9
variables.description: Number of surfaces on the primary tooth which have cavitated caries (d2 lesion), are missing due to caries, and/or have a filling at the age 9 exam (tooth-level decayed or filled surface count).

variables.name: f_sum5
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a filling on the primary tooth at the age 5 exam, regardless of whether or not the surface had separate or recurrent caries lesions.

variables.name: f_sum9
variables.description: Number of surfaces with a filling on the primary tooth at the age 9 exam, regardless of whether or not the surface had separate or recurrent caries lesions.

variables.name: missing_caries5
variables.description: Indicator for whether the primary tooth is missing due to caries at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: missing_caries9
variables.description: Indicator for whether the primary tooth is missing due to caries at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: non_missing_sum5
variables.description: Number of non-missing (scored) surfaces on the primary tooth at the age 5 exam.

variables.name: non_missing_sum9
variables.description: Number of non-missing (scored) surfaces on the primary tooth at the age 9 exam.

tables.description: This dataset contains information on the participants' radial and tibial pQCT cortical bone strength measures. This includes cortical bone axial and polar moments of inertia and X-, Y-, and Polar-Stress-Strain Indices.

variables.name: AGE
variables.description: Age at assessment date (rounded to 0.1 yrs)

variables.name: BONE
variables.description: Tibia or radius bone scan with scan site location (% of bone length is measured from reference line)

variables.name: IP_CM_W
variables.description: Polar moment of inertia of the cortical bone area  (weighted)

variables.name: IXY_CM_W
variables.description: Like IX_CM_W  (weighted)

variables.name: IX_CM_W
variables.description: Axial moment of inertia of the cortical bone area (weighted) Cross Sectional Moment of Inertia for cortical bone

variables.name: IY_CM_W
variables.description: Like IX_CM_W  (weighted)

variables.name: MOVE1
variables.description: Scan quality (movement defined by technician)

variables.name: OBJECTLEN
variables.description: Bone length

variables.name: P_Move_R
variables.description: Proportion of movement (comparison  of cortical bone areas obtained from movement loop)

variables.name: RL
variables.description: Reference line

variables.name: RP_CM_W
variables.description: Moment of resistance = IP_CRT_A/(max distance to the center) (weighted) SSI Polar (stress-strain index)

variables.name: RX_CM_W
variables.description: Moment of resistance = IX_CRT_A/(max distance to y-axis)  (weighted)          SSI X (stress-strain index)

variables.name: RY_CM_W
variables.description: Moment of resistance = IY_CRT_A/(max distance to x-axis)  (weighted)          SSI Y (stress-strain index)

variables.name: SCANID
variables.description: Scan id (identification of scan-same as for image file)

variables.name: SCANNER
variables.description: Scanner used

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Sex

variables.name: SIDE
variables.description: Body side  where limb scan was performed

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: THRESHOLD
variables.description: Constant for all records = 480 mg/cm3 is the default threshold for cortical strength analysis (SSI).

variables.name: WAVE
variables.description: Study assessment wave

variables.name: X_CM_W
variables.description: X-coordinate of the center of cortical area (mean x-coordinate of all Voxels of the cortical area)  (weighted)

variables.name: Y_CM_W
variables.description: Y-coordinate of the center of cortical area (mean y-coordinate of all Voxels of the cortical area)  (weighted)

tables.description: Dental Exams: Tooth-level fluorosis, hypoplasia, and opacity data (primary teeth only)) from the Wave 1 and Wave 2 dental exams. Data includes the number of surfaces which were scored on each tooth, the number of surfaces with fluorosis (TSIF > 0) on each tooth, an indicator for any fluorosis on any surface of the tooth, the number of buccal thirds which were scored on each tooth, the number of buccal thirds with any fluorosis on each tooth, an indicator for any fluorosis on any buccal third of the tooth, and separate indicators for any opacity and hypoplasia.

tables.description: MDCT: Trabecular bone (Tb) and cortical bone (Cb) microstructural measures were computed from MDCT distal tibia scans for subjects at Wave 7 and Wave 9. All Tb measures were computed at 4% to 6% of the distal tibial site over inner (60% peel region) and outer (the annular region between 30% and 60% peels) regions of interest (ROIs), while Cb measures were computed at the 14% to 16% tibial site. Data includes the Wave number, region, volumetric trabecular mineral density, volumetric trabecular mineral density contributed by tranverse trabeculae, volumetric trabecular mineral density contributed by trabecular plates, density of trabecular network area, mean trabecular plate-width, mean trabecular thickness, mean trabecular separation, erosion index, structure model index, mean cortical porosity and cortical thickness.

variables.name: Cb.Poro
variables.description: Mean cortical porosity computed by analyzing BMD over the cortical region

variables.name: Cb.Th
variables.description: Mean cortical thickness computed using a cortical bone segmentation algorithm

variables.name: EI
variables.description: Erosion index - A summary measure of digital topological analysis of trabeculae aimed to represent the extent of bone erosion

variables.name: REGION
variables.description: Region of interest for trabecular bone measures

variables.name: SMI
variables.description: Structure model index - An indicator of the structure of trabeculae; SMI is '0' for perfect parallel plates and '3' for cylindrical rods. Negative values representing concavities were truncated to 0. The rare values greater than 3 represent spherical/blobby structures.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: Tb.NA
variables.description: Density of trabecular network area, i.e., the area of medial surface of segmented trabecular microstructure

variables.name: Tb.PW
variables.description: Mean trabecular plate - width by tensor scale analysis

variables.name: Tb.Sp
variables.description: Mean trabecular separation, i.e., the space between individual trabeculae

variables.name: Tb.Th
variables.description: Mean trabecular thickness

variables.name: Tb.pBMD
variables.description: Volumetric trabecular BMD contributed by trabecular plates

variables.name: Tb.tBMD
variables.description: Volumetric trabecular BMD contributed by transverse trabeculae

variables.name: Tb.vBMD
variables.description: Volumetric trabecular bone mineral density (BMD)

variables.name: WAVE
variables.description: Study assessment wave

tables.description: Demographic / Family Background Assessment: Questionnaire data from the recruitment questionnaire and the Family Demographic Information questionnaire that was given to parents ~2007. The data include the mothers' and fathers' education level and income level at both recruitment and ~2007. In addition, calculated composite socioeconomic status variables at recruitement and ~2007 are included.

variables.name: EDU_F_2007
variables.description: In 2007, what is the highest level of education achieved by the male head of your household? (Family Demographic Information Q3)

variables.name: EDU_F_BASELINE
variables.description: In 1992-95, what is your husband's or partner's highest level of education? (Recruitment Questionnaire Q35)

variables.name: EDU_M_2007
variables.description: In 2007, what is the highest level of education achieved by the female head of your household? (Family Demographic Information Q1)

variables.name: EDU_M_BASELINE
variables.description: In 1992-95, what is your (mother) highest level of education? (Recruitment Questionnaire Q29)

variables.name: INCOME_2007
variables.description: In 2007, which of the following best describes your total household income for the last year before taxes (include salaries, wages, interest, etc.)? (Family Demographic Information Q5)

variables.name: INCOME_BASELINE
variables.description: Which of the following best describes your total household income for the past year before taxes (including salaries, wages, interest, etc.)? (Recruitment Questionnaire Q36)

variables.name: OCCUPATION_F_2007
variables.description: Occupation labels for the male head of household in 2007 (Family Demographic Information Q4)

variables.name: OCCUPATION_M_2007
variables.description: Occupation labels for the female head of household in 2007 (Family Demographic Information Q2)

variables.name: SES_2007
variables.description: Socioeconomic status in 2007

variables.name: SES_BASELINE
variables.description: Socioeconomic status in 1992-95

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

tables.description: Dental Exams: Zone-level fluorosis, hypoplasia, and opacity data (permanent teeth only) from the Wave 2, Wave 4, Wave 6, and Wave 9 dental exams. Data includes the FRI score for each zone and an indicator for definitive fluorosis (FRI >= 2) for each zone.

variables.name: FRI_Score13
variables.description: FRI score for the permanent tooth zone at the age 13 exam.

variables.name: FRI_Score17
variables.description: FRI score for the permanent tooth zone at the age17 exam.

variables.name: FRI_Score23
variables.description: FRI score for the permanent tooth zone at the age 23 exam.

variables.name: FRI_Score9
variables.description: FRI score for the permanent tooth zone at the age 9 exam.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID

variables.name: Tooth
variables.description: Permanent tooth number, ranging from 2 to 31. Although the 3rd molars were scored at the age 23 year exam, they were not not scored for fluorosis. For the age 9 exam, only permanent teeth scored are included in this dataset.

variables.name: Zone
variables.description: Label for permanent tooth zone.

variables.name: definitive_fluorosis_13
variables.description: Indicator for definitive fluorosis on the permanent tooth zone (FRI >= 2) at the age 13 exam.

variables.name: definitive_fluorosis_17
variables.description: Indicator for definitive fluorosis on the permanent tooth zone (FRI >= 2) at the age 17 exam.

variables.name: definitive_fluorosis_23
variables.description: Indicator for definitive fluorosis on the permanent tooth zone (FRI >= 2) at the age 23 exam.

variables.name: definitive_fluorosis_9
variables.description: Indicator for definitive fluorosis on the permanent tooth zone (FRI >= 2) at the age 9 exam.
